original_abstract: Epoxides serve as critical intermediates in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and epoxy resins. In this work, we report a highly efficient photoelectrochemical epoxidation system utilizing a Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediator on an α-Fe₂O₃ photoanode. By using water as the oxygen source, this system achieves exceptional performance across a broad range of alkenes, reaching selectivities exceeding 99% and faradaic efficiencies up to 82 ± 4%—results that significantly outperform previously reported electrochemical and photoelectrochemical methods. Mechanistic investigations confirm that the process follows a Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediated pathway; specifically, Br⁻ undergoes non-radical oxidation to BrO⁻ via oxygen atom transfer on the α-Fe₂O₃ surface, followed by a subsequent oxygen transfer from BrO⁻ to the alkene substrate. The combination of this non-radical mechanism and favorable thermodynamics ensures high catalytic efficiency. This Br⁻/BrO⁻-mediated photoelectrochemical approach offers a sustainable and promising strategy for the simultaneous production of high-value epoxides and green hydrogen.
humanized_abstract: Here, we present a highly efficient photoelectrochemical epoxidation catalyst system for diverse alkenes, employing Br⁻/BrO⁻ as redox mediator on an α-Fe₂O₃ photoanode and water as an oxygen source. The Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediator system affords a high selectivity (selectivities > 99%) and excellent faradaic efficiencies (up to 82 ± 4 %) that far exceed all previous reported electrochemical and photoelectrochemical studies. By identification of a Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediated mechanism with non-radical oxygen atom transfer (OAT) to oxidize Br⁻ to BrO⁻ on the photoanode and subsequent selective transfer of O from BrO⁻ to the unsaturated bond of alkenes, this Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediated photoelectrochemical system provides an environmentally friendly pathway to simultaneously produce valuable-oxidized products, such as desired chemical products of high commercial value, like various type of epoxides, along with green hydrogen, as a renewable energy carrier.

The Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediator system affords a high selectivity (selectivities > 99%) and excellent faradaic efficiencies (up to 82 ± 4 %) that far exceed all previous reported electrochemical and photoelectrochemical studies. By identification of a Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediated mechanism with non-radical oxygen atom transfer (OAT) to oxidize Br⁻ to BrO⁻ on the photoanode and subsequent selective transfer of O from BrO⁻ to the unsaturated bond of alkenes, this Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediated photoelectrochemical system provides an environmentally friendly pathway to simultaneously produce valuable-oxidized products, such as desired chemical products of high commercial value, like various type of epoxides, along with green hydrogen, as a renewable energy carrier.
